title: HTML Link Semantics, Base URLs, Relations, and Discovery Decisions · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs html link semantics, base urls, relations, and discovery decisions from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for semantic link-discovery ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Enumerate link-bearing HTML elements and distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, form, media, and embedded destinations.
Apply first-valid-base rules against the final response URL and retain base source and rejected alternatives.
Interpret rel tokens, download, target, media, hreflang, canonical, alternate, and nofollow under an explicit discovery policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Discovery Provenance case record

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 4906 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify link-bearing elements, base and relation semantics, rejected references, and discovery provenance; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain source page, element, attribute, raw reference, resolved URL, relation tokens, scope decision, and frontier result. Artifact under review: semantic link-discovery ledger.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Discovery Provenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain source page, element, attribute, raw reference, resolved URL, relation tokens, scope decision, and frontier result.
